generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Permanent magnet synchronous motor (PMSM) providing high efficiency and torque density for precise motion control in the industrial robot system"
]

instance CycloidalGearbox : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Cycloidal gearbox providing precise positional control and torque amplification for the actuation system"
]

instance SINUMERIK840DSlController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Core Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl controller enabling real-time control and bidirectional communication via MQTT protocol"
]

// C3: Physical sensing components
instance MotorPositionEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position encoder mounted directly on the permanent magnet synchronous motor for real-time feedback monitoring of displacement"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorPositionData
]

instance GearboxPositionEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position encoder mounted behind the cycloidal gearbox for spatial distribution and precise positional measurement"
    DTDFVocab:producedData GearboxPositionData
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance MotorPositionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorPositionEncoder
]

instance GearboxPositionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom GearboxPositionEncoder
]

instance InputCurrentData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorPositionEncoder
]

instance TorqueData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorPositionEncoder
]

instance AngularVelocityData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorPositionEncoder
]

// C5: Data aggregation and contextualization
instance ContextualDigitalShadow : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated contextual data traces from physical sensors (input current, torque, angular velocity) processed through digital shadow principles for manufacturing analysis"
    DTDFVocab:fromData InputCurrentData, TorqueData, AngularVelocityData
]

instance PreprocessedSensorTraces : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Abstracted and preprocessed sensor data traces for noise reduction, normalization, and contextual enrichment before DT analysis"
    DTDFVocab:fromData ContextualDigitalShadow
]

// C10: Models
instance AASArchitectureModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Standardized Asset Administration Shell (AAS) framework integrating robot components (motors, encoders, controllers) for unified digital representation and interoperability"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SINUMERIK840DSlController
]

instance SysMLComponentModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML component and connector modeling for architecture description of robot system components and their interconnections"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MBSETools
]

instance SysMLStateMachineModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML state machines for detailed behavior modeling of robot system operations and control logic"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MBSETools
]

instance SimulinkBehaviorModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink model for behavior simulation and validation of robot control algorithms and system dynamics"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MBSETools
]

// C11: Enablers
instance MQTTProtocol : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Lightweight publish-subscribe messaging protocol enabling bidirectional data flow between physical robot and Digital Twin with low-latency contextual data aggregation"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ContextualDigitalShadow
]

instance MBSETools : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Model-Based Systems Engineering tools (SysML for architecture description and MontiArc for state machine validation) enabling systematic DT modeling and validation"
    DTDFVocab:enables AASArchitectureModel, SysMLComponentModel, SysMLStateMachineModel
]

instance SINUMERIK840DSlController : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Core Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl controller providing real-time control capabilities and embedded simulation engines for closed-loop validation"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeSimulationEngine
]

instance RealTimeSimulationEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Embedded real-time simulation engines in SINUMERIK 840D sl enabling closed-loop validation of DT predictions and control algorithm testing"
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionIdentificationService
]

// C6: Services
instance FrictionIdentificationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous, multi-physics-aware friction identification service using aggregated contextual data traces from robot sensors"
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionLossDetectionInsight
]

instance FrictionLossDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service detecting anomalies in friction-related parameters (torque, velocity deviations) to ensure motion accuracy and prevent faults"
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionAnomalyDetectionInsight
]

instance VisualizationDashboardService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "User interface service for monitoring friction-related metrics, enabling operators to visualize system health and performance trends"
    DTDFVocab:provides OperationalPerformanceDashboard
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance FrictionLossDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Detection of abnormal friction loss patterns in robot joint operations, enabling predictive maintenance actions"
]

instance FrictionAnomalyDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identification of torque and velocity deviations indicating potential friction-related faults in robot actuation system"
]

instance OperationalPerformanceDashboard : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Visual representation of real-time and historical friction metrics for maintenance technicians and production supervisors"
]

instance MaintenanceAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated maintenance action triggered by detected friction anomalies to prevent system failures"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance PerformanceOptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Manual or automated performance optimization action based on dashboard insights to improve robot system efficiency"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The **Digital Twin (DT) system** is conceptualized as an extension of the **Digital Shadow**, which operates as a **one-way data flow** from the physical system to a digital representation. The **orchestration** of the DT system is grounded in **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies, leveraging **Architecture Description Languages (ADLs)** like **MontiArc Automaton** or **SysML state machines** to model behavior. The **system-wide coordination** is implicitly supported by **active software components** that process data from digital shadows, enabling autonomous operations and real-time synchronization. The **resource management** is implied through the integration of **simulation tools** (e.g., **MathWorks Simulink**) and **general-purpose languages** (e.g., **C++/Java**) to refine and validate DT models. The absence of explicit orchestration patterns (e.g., microservices, event-driven architectures) in the documents suggests a **hybrid centralized-decentralized approach**, where core coordination is handled by MBSE frameworks and ADLs, supplemented by tool-specific workflows."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
  A Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle in the provided context spans structured modeling phases aligned with **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies, particularly emphasizing **multi-level abstraction** (requirements, functional architecture, logical architecture, and product/technical layers). Key stages include:
  - **Development Phase**: Utilizes **SysML** and **Architecture Description Languages (ADLs)** like **ACME, AADL, MontiArc**, or **SysML IBDs** to define components, interfaces, and interconnections via component & connector perspectives. **Behavioral modeling** is supported through **MontiArc Automata** or **state machines** in SysML v1.7, often integrated with simulation tools such as **MathWorks Simulink** or general-purpose languages like **C++/Java**.
  - **Data Aggregation & Abstraction**: Digital Shadows (DS) serve as foundational data traces, aggregating contextual information for specific purposes, while DTs enable **active processing** (e.g., software components interpreting data) to evolve beyond passive DS roles.
  **Transition Management**: The shift from DS to DT involves integrating **active components** (e.g., software systems) to enable bidirectional data flow, governance likely follows **MBSE methodologies** (e.g., RFLP, OOSEM, SMArDT) to ensure alignment across heterogeneous stakeholders’ perspectives.
  **Lifecycle Governance**: Emphasizes **heterogeneous stakeholder integration** and **multi-tool/model notation compatibility** (e.g., SysML + Simulink + C++/Java) to maintain consistency across abstraction layers.
 "
]

    base:desc "
  The provided documents reference **architecture description languages (ADLs)** such as **ACME, AADL, MontiArc, and SysML IBDs**, which define structural and behavioral models for Digital Twin systems. **Asset Administration Shells (AAS)** are mentioned in the broader context (Abstract) but are not explicitly detailed in the extracted sections. **Interoperability standards** are implied through ADLs but not formally specified. **Certification requirements** or compliance frameworks (e.g., ISO/IEC standards) are **not addressed**, and no explicit interoperability protocols (e.g., OPC UA) are mentioned.

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system incorporates a **physical safety mechanism** for robotic operations, specifically leveraging a **safety cell** that restricts the operational range of the robotic arm (e.g., MABI Max-100) to a defined angular workspace of **[−90°, 90°]**, ensuring operator safety by preventing unintended collisions. While the provided documents do not explicitly detail cybersecurity protocols, the system’s integration with **IoT platforms** (e.g., Thingsboard) implies potential exposure to network-based threats, necessitating adherence to **MQTT protocol** (as referenced in Document 52) for secure communication between devices. The system’s compliance with **RAMI 4.0** (Document 48, 49) suggests adherence to safety-critical standards, though no explicit mention of **ISO 13849** or other cybersecurity frameworks is documented. The Digital Twin’s reliance on **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)** (Document 50) for asset modeling may also imply standardized safety and security governance frameworks, though specific details on fail-safe mechanisms or threat mitigation are absent."
]
